title: **Littleborough: A Scenic Town with Rich History and Outdoor Attractions**

content_html:
Littleborough, located within the borough of Rochdale, lies at the foothills of the scenic South Pennines. This historic town served as an Urban District from 1894 until its incorporation into Rochdale in 1974.

A key highlight for visitors and residents alike is Hollingworth Lake, a popular destination for various water sports, including rowing, sailing, and windsurfing. The lake draws tourists year-round, contributing to the local economy and promoting outdoor activities.

The town centre, characterised by its stone-built architecture, is designated as a conservation area due to its unique architectural qualities. This status helps preserve Littleborough's heritage and charm, making it an attractive place for both locals and visitors.

As Littleborough continues to blend its rich history with modern amenities, it remains a vibrant community in the heart of Greater Manchester, offering a mix of recreational activities and cultural heritage.

title: **Heywood: A Historical Mill Town with a Unique Nickname**
excerpt: Heywood, a mill town located in Greater Manchester, has a rich history dating back to the Industrial Revolution. With a population of approximately 30,000 residents, the town is often affectionately r
content_html:
Heywood, a mill town located in Greater Manchester, has a rich history dating back to the Industrial Revolution. With a population of approximately 30,000 residents, the town is often affectionately referred to as 'Monkey Town'. This nickname is believed to stem from the local pronunciation of Heap Bridge, which sounds like 'ape bridge'.

In 1974, Heywood became part of the Rochdale Metropolitan Borough, further integrating its historical significance into the region. The town's name is linked to two notable figures in British history. The first, Peter Heywood, played a crucial role in thwarting the Gunpowder Plot by arresting Guy Fawkes. The second, also named Peter Heywood, was a member of the infamous mutiny aboard HMS Bounty in 1789.

Today, Heywood continues to celebrate its industrial heritage while evolving as a vibrant community within Rochdale.

title: **Seven Wonders of Greater Manchester: A Heritage Tour Proposal**
excerpt: Greater Manchester boasts a wealth of history, natural beauty, and unique architecture, yet lacks a dedicated cross-city heritage tour. In response, a proposal for a 'Seven Wonders of Greater Manchest
content_html:
Greater Manchester boasts a wealth of history, natural beauty, and unique architecture, yet lacks a dedicated cross-city heritage tour. In response, a proposal for a 'Seven Wonders of Greater Manchester' has emerged, aiming to highlight the region's intriguing sites.

Among the proposed wonders is Stockport's Pyramid, a distinctive ziggurat structure that has stood since 1992. Initially part of a series of pyramid-shaped buildings along the River Mersey, it served as a Co-op store until its closure at the end of the last decade. Efforts to revitalise the landmark have yet to come to fruition, but it remains a notable sight for drivers on the M60.

Another suggested wonder is the Four Pillars of the Rochdale Canal, located between Albion Lock and Tib Lock. These four columns, three of which are partially collapsed and covered in foliage, are remnants of warehouses that once lined the canal. Although not widely recognised, they are steeped in mystery and history, prompting curiosity about their past.

Local residents are encouraged to share their thoughts on what should be included in the Seven Wonders of Greater Manchester as the initiative gains traction.

title: **Rochdale to Host International Mural Festival 'Common Walls' This September**
excerpt: Rochdale will be transformed into a vibrant open-air gallery from 7 to 14 September as the international mural festival, Common Walls, takes place in the town centre. The festival will feature ten lar
content_html:
Rochdale will be transformed into a vibrant open-air gallery from 7 to 14 September as the international mural festival, Common Walls, takes place in the town centre. The festival will feature ten large-scale murals created by renowned artists from around the globe, each inspired by Rochdale’s rich history and culture.

Festival-goers will have the unique opportunity to watch these world-class artists at work during the week-long event. All murals will be located within a 30-minute walk of each other, with a map available to help visitors navigate the colourful displays.

Among the artists participating are Insane51, Onur, Smug1, SecaOne, AYLO, Epod, and Lei Mai, each known for their impactful contributions to street art. Lead artist and curator, Hayley Garner, also known as AYLO, expressed her excitement about bringing her craft to her hometown during Rochdale's year as Greater Manchester Town of Culture.

AYLO, who is part of the acclaimed duo Nomad Clan, stated, “I’ve painted murals all over the world, but nothing compares to painting in your hometown alongside artists I have looked up to.” The festival promises to be a celebration of creativity, connection, and community spirit in Rochdale.

title: **Rochdale Grooming Gang Members Face Possible Deportation**
excerpt: Two members of a Rochdale grooming gang, jailed 13 years ago for serious crimes against children, may soon be deported to Pakistan, according to recent reports. This development follows local MP Paul
content_html:
Two members of a Rochdale grooming gang, jailed 13 years ago for serious crimes against children, may soon be deported to Pakistan, according to recent reports. This development follows local MP Paul Waugh's expression of "utter outrage" over the pair's successful efforts to resist multiple deportation attempts.

Adil Khan, 54, and Abdul Rauf, 55, were part of a gang convicted in 2012 for exploiting vulnerable girls. Khan, who was sentenced to eight years, was involved in the trafficking of young girls, including one he impregnated. He was released on licence after serving four years. Rauf, sentenced to six years, trafficked a 15-year-old girl, using his taxi to transport her to secluded locations for sexual exploitation. He was released after two and a half years.

Both men reportedly remain in Rochdale, with Rauf currently working for a takeaway delivery service. Greater Manchester's deputy mayor, Kate Green, previously raised concerns about the situation with Home Secretary Yvette Cooper.

The Telegraph has indicated that Pakistani officials are nearing a decision regarding the deportation of Khan and Rauf, as negotiations are underway to resolve a blockage on their return. Despite previous legal challenges and appeals against deportation, both men may soon face removal from the UK, following the Home Office's efforts to revoke their British citizenship.

title: **£1.9 Million Investment to Enhance Rochdale’s Electricity Infrastructure**
excerpt: A £1.9 million project to upgrade Rochdale's electricity network is currently in progress. SP Electricity North West, the power network operator, has initiated the work as part of its proactive invest
content_html:
A £1.9 million project to upgrade Rochdale's electricity network is currently in progress. SP Electricity North West, the power network operator, has initiated the work as part of its proactive investment programme. The project will involve the replacement of three kilometres of underground cables and updates to various substation assets.

Spanning from Belfield to Gale, this initiative aims to enhance capacity and reliability for thousands of customers in Rochdale and the surrounding areas. Paul Riley, who is managing the project, emphasised the importance of the upgrade, stating, “This upgrade is a vital step in modernising our network and supporting the region’s future energy needs.”

The project includes the replacement of ageing oil-filled cables with modern technology, which is expected to improve reliability while minimising environmental risks. SP Electricity North West is collaborating closely with Rochdale Council to ensure that access for both businesses and residents remains uninterrupted during the works.

Temporary traffic lights will be implemented on Park Road, Bond Street, and Yorkshire Road to facilitate safe working conditions for engineers. Customers have been informed of the ongoing work through hand-delivered letters. Construction teams have commenced work on Park Road and will subsequently progress to March Street, Belfield Road, Whitworth Road, and Gale Street.

title: **Landlords Face Urgent Deadline as Awaab’s Law Takes Effect Next Month**
excerpt: Landlords in Rochdale are being urged to prepare for Awaab’s Law, which comes into force next month, imposing new legal duties to address damp and mould hazards in social housing. This legislation fol
content_html:
Landlords in Rochdale are being urged to prepare for Awaab’s Law, which comes into force next month, imposing new legal duties to address damp and mould hazards in social housing. This legislation follows the tragic death of two-year-old Awaab Ishak, who passed away in December 2020 due to a severe respiratory illness linked to prolonged exposure to black mould in his home.

Under Awaab’s Law, social landlords must investigate reports of damp and mould and rectify emergency hazards within 24 hours. George Edwards, Managing Director of Timberwise, emphasised the importance of proactive measures, stating that many property owners remain unaware of these forthcoming obligations. He described the law as a significant step in addressing the growing health risks associated with untreated damp and mould issues.

Edwards advised landlords and housing associations to conduct regular property inspections and maintenance to prevent problems from escalating. While the current legislation applies to social housing, the Government has indicated plans to extend these requirements to the private rented sector through the upcoming Renter’s Rights Bill.

As tenants gain more power to hold landlords accountable, it is essential that they report damp and mould issues promptly to ensure effective solutions are implemented.

Captain Ethan Ebanks-Landell opened the scoring in the 13th minute, heading home after Mani Dieseruvwe redirected a Joe Pritchard cross. The lead doubled just before half-time when Dieseruvwe found the net, finishing off a well-placed pass from Devante Rodney.

Rodney himself added a third goal in the 70th minute, tapping in after Aidan Barlow's initial shot was saved by the Gateshead goalkeeper. Substitute Connor McBride rounded off the scoring in the 79th minute, securing a comprehensive win for Rochdale.

This victory marks a significant boost for Rochdale as they aim to maintain their position at the top of the league. The team will look to build on this momentum in their upcoming fixtures.

Upon investigation, it was found that the figure lacks sufficient context and does not reflect the overall voting patterns in the constituency. While Galloway, representing the Workers Party of Britain, did receive a notable share of the postal votes, the claim exaggerates the actual percentage. Electoral data indicates that while he performed well, other candidates also secured substantial support from postal voters.

Local election officials have urged voters to rely on verified information and official results rather than unsubstantiated claims circulating on social media. The full breakdown of the election results, including postal voting statistics, is expected to be released in the coming days, providing a clearer picture of the electoral landscape in Rochdale.

Residents are encouraged to stay informed through official channels as the local political scene continues to develop.

content_html:
A disturbing new report has shed light on the ongoing issues surrounding grooming gangs in Rochdale, detailing horrific accounts of abuse suffered by young girls between 2004 and 2012. The report outlines cases of extreme exploitation, including a teenage girl who died from a heroin overdose after being injected by an abuser, and another child forced to act like a dog while kept in a cage.

The report identifies 96 men still considered a potential risk to children, prompting whistleblowers to assert that grooming continues in the area. This follows previous findings from a 2013 report, which revealed that police and social services may have neglected the issue due to fears of being perceived as racist, despite evidence that young, white girls were being targeted by predominantly Asian gangs.

Sara Rowbotham, a whistleblower and former sexual health worker, expressed her anguish over the ongoing situation, questioning how many more reports or public outcries will be needed for accountability. Rowbotham, who has been recognised for her advocacy, emphasised the need for systemic change to protect vulnerable children.

The report serves as a sobering reminder of the urgent need for continued vigilance and action to safeguard the youth of Rochdale from exploitation.

title: **Rochdale's Unbeaten Start Ends with Defeat to Brackley Town**

content_html:

The hosts took an early lead in the 14th minute when Ryan Haynes, a former Newport defender, scored with a curling free-kick that found the top corner.

Rochdale equalised shortly after the break, with Connor McBride finding the net in the 52nd minute, thanks to an assist from substitute Anthony Mancini. However, Brackley regained the lead just after the hour mark when Connor Hall, a former Sheffield United youth player, converted the rebound after his initial penalty was saved by Rochdale’s teenage goalkeeper, Tom Myles.

Despite pressing for an equaliser, Rochdale was unable to find the back of the net again, marking a disappointing end to their strong start. The defeat leaves Rochdale looking to regroup ahead of their next fixture as they aim to return to winning ways in the National League.

title: **Fatal Methanol Poisoning Highlights Risks of Contaminated Alcohol**

content_html:
A tragic incident involving a Rochdale woman has raised awareness about the dangers of methanol poisoning. Kirsty McKie, 38, passed away in 2022 after unknowingly consuming contaminated alcohol while in Bali.

Kirsty, who was on holiday, suffered a fatal reaction after drinking a locally produced alcoholic beverage that contained high levels of methanol, a toxic substance often found in illicit spirits. Her family has since urged others to be cautious when consuming alcohol abroad, particularly in regions where regulations may be lax.

Methanol poisoning can lead to severe health complications and even death, with symptoms that may initially resemble those of a hangover. Authorities have warned that such incidents can occur to anyone, regardless of their location or experience with alcohol.

Kirsty's family is now advocating for greater awareness of the risks associated with drinking unregulated alcohol, especially in tourist destinations. They hope that sharing her story will help prevent similar tragedies in the future and encourage travellers to make informed choices about their beverages.

According to the mother, her daughter has a life-threatening allergy to peanuts, and she expressed concern over the airline’s lack of preparedness in handling such situations. She stated that despite informing the crew about her daughter’s condition prior to take-off, they initially appeared reluctant to take action.

The mother described the experience as distressing, highlighting the potential dangers faced by individuals with allergies when airlines do not have clear protocols in place. She is now advocating for better training for airline staff to ensure they are equipped to manage allergy-related issues effectively.

In response to the incident, the airline has stated that passenger safety is a top priority and that they will review their allergy policies to ensure appropriate measures are taken in the future. The mother hopes that her experience will lead to improved awareness and procedures for managing food allergies on flights.

title: **Salford Council to Consider Sale of Affordable Homes at Market Value**

content_html:
Salford City Council is set to review a proposal that would see 42 planned affordable homes sold at market value, part of a larger £2.5 billion housing scheme. Originally intended to provide low-cost housing options, the adjustment has raised concerns among local residents and housing advocates about the potential impact on affordability in the area.

The decision comes as part of a broader strategy to address housing shortages in Salford, where demand for affordable housing continues to outstrip supply. Council officials argue that selling these homes at market value could generate necessary funds for future development projects and enhance the overall housing stock in the region.

However, critics of the plan express apprehension that this move could exacerbate the existing housing crisis, making it increasingly difficult for low-income families to find suitable accommodation. The council will discuss the proposal in an upcoming meeting, where community feedback is expected to play a crucial role in the final decision.

Frith's comments come after numerous complaints from families who have been penalised during brief pick-up or drop-off visits.

In a statement, Frith emphasised the need for urgent reform to the airport's parking regulations, highlighting that many users are being caught out by unclear instructions and inadequate information. He expressed that the current system is disproportionately affecting those who only require short stays at the airport.

The issue has sparked a debate among local residents, with some voicing their frustrations on social media. Comments have ranged from dissatisfaction with the airport's management to concerns about the financial implications for local councils, which hold a stake in the Manchester Airport Group.

Frith's call for action aims to alleviate the burden on families and ensure that the parking system is fair and transparent for all users. The airport has yet to respond to the calls for change.

title: **Heywood: A Historic Mill Town with a Unique Nickname**
excerpt: Heywood, a mill town located in Greater Manchester, has a rich history that dates back to the industrial revolution. With a population of approximately 30,000, the town became part of Rochdale in 1974
content_html:
Heywood, a mill town located in Greater Manchester, has a rich history that dates back to the industrial revolution. With a population of approximately 30,000, the town became part of Rochdale in 1974.

Affectionately known as 'Monkey Town', the nickname is believed to stem from the area's original name, Heap Bridge, which sounds like 'ape bridge' in the local accent.

The Heywood family has played a significant role in British history. Notably, Peter Heywood was instrumental in thwarting the Gunpowder Plot by arresting Guy Fawkes. Another Peter Heywood made headlines in 1789 as a mutineer aboard HMS Bounty, marking key moments in the nation’s past.

Today, Heywood continues to celebrate its industrial heritage while embracing modern developments, making it a vibrant community within the Rochdale borough.

title: **Seven Wonders of Greater Manchester: A New Heritage Tour Proposal**
excerpt: Greater Manchester's rich history, natural beauty, and architectural uniqueness have inspired the idea of a heritage tour highlighting the region's "Seven Wonders." While the vastness of Greater Manch
content_html:
Greater Manchester's rich history, natural beauty, and architectural uniqueness have inspired the idea of a heritage tour highlighting the region's "Seven Wonders." While the vastness of Greater Manchester presents practical challenges for such a tour, the proposal aims to showcase a blend of intriguing sights.

Among the proposed wonders is the Stockport Pyramid, officially a ziggurat, which has stood as a landmark on the M60 since its opening in 1992. Once home to the Co-op, the structure has been vacant since the end of the last decade, although plans for revitalisation have been discussed.

Another noteworthy inclusion is the Four Pillars of the Rochdale Canal, located between Albion Lock and Tib Lock. These columns, three of which are partially collapsed and adorned with greenery, are believed to have once supported warehouses that have since been demolished. Their mysterious history adds to the intrigue of the canal, which is a significant feature of the local landscape.

As the idea of the Seven Wonders of Greater Manchester gains traction, local residents are encouraged to share their thoughts on what should be included in this unique heritage tour.
